config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu adalah batas agar negara atau mayoritas tidak bertindak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya, kalau “demi masyarakat” dipakai terlalu luas, itu sering jadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, kritik, dan kelompok minoritas. Yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically outrank the individual, because in the United States that kind of logic has often been used to excuse government overreach and to sideline unpopular minorities. Public safety, public health, and shared institutions matter, but they do not justify treating individual rights as disposable whenever the majority wants something. From my perspective, rights are exactly what keep "the greater good" from becoming a blank check for coercion. The better principle is that society can set limits, but only with strong justification and real protections for individual liberty.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi saya menolak kalau itu dijadikan alasan untuk mengalahkan hak individu secara otomatis. Di Indonesia, saya sering melihat dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, entah terhadap kebebasan berbicara, kelompok kecil, atau warga yang lemah posisinya. Hak individu justru perlu dijaga supaya mayoritas dan negara tidak mudah semena-mena, meski dalam situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, pembatasan bisa saja sah kalau benar-benar perlu dan proporsional. Jadi, bagi saya, yang utama bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang ketat dengan perlindungan hak yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, that principle has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the majority claims it is acting for the "common good." I do think society can impose limits for things like public health and safety, but those limits need a very high bar and strong protections so they do not swallow basic rights. To me, the real test of a just society is whether it can protect the public without treating individual liberty as expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal justru hak individu dibutuhkan untuk melindungi warga dari kesewenang-wenangan mayoritas dan negara. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi pembatasannya harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Jadi, yang saya anggap benar bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan yang tegas dengan perlindungan hak yang kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when leaders claim they are acting for the common good. I do think there are moments when society can limit individual freedom for public health or safety, but those limits need a very high standard and serious safeguards. To me, a society is only legitimate when it protects the public without making personal rights easy to discard.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
